compiled_state.title: Reducer fork tie-break is filename/replay-order, not UUID as documented
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why
The SDET review (8125) found that the reconciler reducer fork/union tie-break does NOT resolve by event UUID as documented. CLAUDE.md / the reducer docstring state the STATUS fork is "resolved deterministically by UUID". Empirically (test_concurrency_regression fork test), for the concurrent-transition case both competing STATUS siblings carry an empty parent_status_uuid, so the second-applied event forks against an empty existing_uuid and wins UNCONDITIONALLY — i.e. the winner is the later-in-replay-order event (filename = timestamp-then-uuid order), NOT the UUID-determined one. Clones still CONVERGE (identical union replayed in identical filename order -> same winner), so the cross-clone agreement guarantee holds; but the documented MECHANISM ("by UUID") is inaccurate.

## Impact
Not a convergence/correctness break (clones agree). It is a doctrine-vs-implementation discrepancy: anyone relying on "lower/higher UUID wins" would be wrong, and the tie-break is timestamp-order-sensitive. test_concurrency_regression now pins the ACTUAL behavior (max by filename) and documents the divergence inline.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Decide intended semantics: either (a) make the reducer tie-break the STATUS fork by event UUID (so the doc is true), or (b) correct the docs/CLAUDE.md to describe the actual filename/replay-order tie-break (and confirm convergence is still guaranteed for clones whose pre-union event orderings differ)
- [ ] Reconcile the reducer code, its docstring, and CLAUDE.md to ONE accurate description
- [ ] test_concurrency_regression asserts the (then-correct) documented winner; fails if the rule changes
